summary: Crystal‑clear matrix recording captures a jam‑heavy show highlighted by a monster Scarlet > Fire and an epic, extended Franklin’s Tower.
review: The matrix source delivers excellent clarity and instrument separation, letting Phil’s fiery bass and Brent’s keyboards shine (even if the Casio was a bit loud at times). Musically the concert is strong – long, flowing jams with a beastly Scarlet > Fire and a marathon Franklin’s Tower stand out, though the latter part of the second set feels a little less compelling to some listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and recording quality; only minor criticisms about keyboard volume and later‑set pacing keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, full‑bodied sound with excellent instrument separation; drums captured well. One reviewer notes Brent's Casio 1000 was a bit too loud in spots.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls strong with tight jams; second set features massive stretches (notably Scarlet > Fire and a 15‑minute Franklin’s Tower) but the later portion feels less engaging to some listeners.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Stretching songs out to accommodate Brent's keyboards, giving the set a more exploratory feel.
band_member_comments.Phil: On fire this night; his bass is prominent and vibrant throughout the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboard work is described as 'nuts' and inspiring, though at times the Casio 1000 volume was too high.
band_member_comments.Drums: Recorded cleanly and sit well in the mix.
